market_overview: Market sentiment is highly focused on a landmark AI Data Center collaboration between SNS Network and Gabungan AQRS. This 'Silicon Valley' thematic is driving capital into Technology and Infrastructure, offsetting profit-taking in the broader semiconductor segment.

top_picks.name: SNS
top_picks.analysis: SNS has surged +4.35% following the signing of an MoU with GBGAQRS to develop an AI Data Center. Its role as the hardware provider for NVIDIA super-servers makes it a key beneficiary of the 'AI Factory' cycle.

deep_dive.thesis: Transition to digital infrastructure. SNS is shifting from a hardware reseller to a GPU-as-a-Service (GPUaaS) provider, which commands significantly higher valuation multiples.
catalysts: AI Data Center MoU with GBGAQRS
risks: Project execution and land acquisition delays

top_picks.name: GBGAQRS
top_picks.analysis: GBGAQRS is moving in tandem with SNS (+2.86%). As the civil engineering partner for the new AI Data Center project, the company successfully diversifies its order book away from traditional highways.

deep_dive.thesis: Infrastructure pivot. By partnering with SNS, AQRS enters the high-margin data center construction space, complementing its upcoming cash flow boost from LRT3 handover.
catalysts: AI Data Center infrastructure contract potential

top_picks.name: INARI
top_picks.analysis: Inari is undergoing a technical consolidation (-1.52%) following its recent rally. Long-term RF segment growth remains robust as the Lumileds termination clears the balance sheet path.

deep_dive.thesis: Strategic de-risking remains the core play. Current price levels offer a low-risk entry for institutions looking to increase tech exposure for 2H2026.

top_picks.name: GAMUDA
top_picks.analysis: Gamuda is seeing minor profit-taking (-1.13%). Despite the dip, it remains the dominant large-cap construction proxy with a massive order book target of RM50b by end of 2026.

deep_dive.thesis: Regional engineering giant. Gamuda's success in Taiwan and Australia provides a hedge against domestic infrastructure delays.
catalysts: Data center contract wins in PD

top_picks.name: TENAGA
top_picks.analysis: Stable utility play. TNB is the ultimate beneficiary of the SNS-AQRS data center news, as every new server farm requires massive grid connectivity and power off-take.

deep_dive.thesis: Grid infrastructure monopoly. TNB is no longer a utility; it is a digital transformation enabler with a 4% dividend yield floor.
